title: Merchant Shipping (Counting and Registration of Persons on Board Passenger Ships) Regulations, 2011
title_en: Merchant Shipping (Counting and Registration of Persons on Board Passenger Ships) Regulations, 2011

provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Merchant shipping (Counting and Registration of Persons on Board Passenger Ships) Regulations, 2011.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: (1) These Regulations shall apply to any— (a) Tanzanian passenger ship wherever it may be; (b) Other passenger ship while within Tanzanian waters . (2) These Regulations shall not apply to any— (a) ship of war; (b) troop ship; or (c) pleasure vessel unless certificated to carry more than twelve passengers for commercial purposes.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: (1) In these Regulations unless the context require otherwise— " Act " means the Merchant Shipping Act ; [Cap. 165] " Authority " means the Surface and Marine Transport Regulatory Authority; " appropriate search and rescue services " means in relation to a ship involved in an emergency, the search and rescue services for the area in which the ship is located; " community ship " means a ship which is registered in, or entitled to fly the flag of a member State of the East African Community; " international voyage " means a voyage from a country to which the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea applies to a port outside that country, or conversely; " landing point " means any berth, excursion point, floating pier or stage, link span, pier, port, stop or anchorage point from or to where passengers are embarked or disembarked; " merchant shipping notice " means a notice described, issued by the Surface and Marine Transport Regulatory Authority and any particular document amending or replacing that Notice; " mile " means a nautical mile of 1852 metres; " Minister " means the Minister responsible for shipping; " Owner " means in respect of a registered ship, the registered owner and includes a demise charterer and a managing owner or a managing agent; " passenger " means any person carried on a ship except— (a) a person employed or engaged in any capacity on the business of the Ship ; (b) a person on board the ship either in pursuance of the obligation laid upon the master to carry shipwrecked, distressed or other persons, or by reason if any circumstance that neither the master nor the owner nor the charterer, could have prevented or forestalled. (c) A child under one year of age; " passenger registrar " means a person appointed by an owner of a ship pursuant to regulation 9(1)(b) of these Regulations; " passenger ship " means a ship which is constructed for, or which is habitually or on any particular occasion used for carrying more than twelve passengers and includes a ship that is provided for the transport or entertainment of lodgers at any institution, hotel, boarding house, guest house or other establishment; " person " means any person on board in a ship irrespective of his age; " pleasure vessel " means any vessel which is defined as pleasure vessel under the Act ; " protected sea or lake area " means a sea or lake area— (a) sheltered from open sea or lake; (b) where a ship is at no time more than six miles from a place of refuge and shipwrecked persons can land; and (c) the proximity of search and rescue facilities is ensured. " Registrar of Ships " means a person appointed as Registrar of Ships pursuant to section 7 of the Act; " regular community service " means a regular service in a sea or lake area where the annual probability of the significant wave height exceeding two metres is less than ten percent and the voyage does not exceed thirty miles or thereabouts from the point of departure; or the primary purpose of the service is to provide regular links to outlying communities for customary purposes; " regular service " means a series of ship crossings operated so as to serve traffic between the same two or more ports either— (a) according to a published timetable; or (b) with crossings so regular or frequent that they constitute a recognizable systematic series; " search and rescue services " means the search and rescue services responsible for the initiation and co-ordination of all maritime search and rescue activities required to provide assistance in the event of an emergency or its aftermath; " Ship " includes every description of vessel used in navigation; " Tanzania passenger ship " means a passenger ship which is a Tanzanian ship ; " Tanzanian ship " means a ship registered or licensed under the provisions of the Act and any other Act at a port in the United Republic of Tanzania; " Tanzanian waters " means the sea or other waters within the sea ward limits of the territorial sea of Tanzania; " voyage " means the whole distance between the ship port or place of departure and her final port or place of arrival. Part II – Counting and registration systems

provisions.title: Passenger counting
provisions.content: (1) An owner of a Tanzania passenger ship shall establish a system of counting persons on board before leaving any landing point . (2) An owner of a non- Tanzania passenger ship shall ensure that there is in place a system capable of counting persons on board before leaving any landing point in the United Republic of Tanzania. (3) The established system of counting persons shall be such that— (a) all persons boarding a ship at a landing point at the beginning of a voyage are counted and named individually on or prior to boarding as the case may be; (b) all persons disembarking at subsequent landing points during the course of a voyage , are counted individually as they disembark; (c) all persons boarding at subsequent landing points, during the course of a voyage , are counted individually on, or just prior to, boarding; and (d) the number of persons remaining on board at each landing point is determined. (4) Where the counting system established in respect of a passenger ship pursuant to sub regulation (1) includes a system of individual passenger boarding cards, a passenger shall not be permitted to board the ship unless he has been issued with an individual boarding card.

provisions.title: Communication of information
provisions.content: (1) The total number of persons on board determined immediately before a passenger ship leaves any landing point by means of the counting system shall be communicated to the master of the ship and the passenger registrar . (2) The owner of a ship shall ensure that there is a system of recording and communicating information to the master of a ship where a person on board a ship has declared a need for special care or assistance in emergency situations.

provisions.title: Restriction on departure
provisions.content: A passenger ship shall not leave any landing point if— (a) the requirements of regulation (9) have not been complied with; (b) the total number of persons on board the ship exceeds the number of persons the ship is permitted to carry; (c) no plan is available on board on how to deal with passengers in an emergency situation or no passenger emergency drill has been carried out during the preceding six months.

provisions.title: Counting system to be on board and approved by the Registrar
provisions.content: (1) The counting system established pursuant to the requirements of regulation 3(1) shall be in form of written instructions which shall be kept on board at all times in the custody of the master. (2) The counting system shall be submitted to the Registrar of Ships for approval.

provisions.title: Additional requirement for voyages
provisions.content: (1) An owner of any passenger ship of voyages of more than twenty miles shall comply with the following additional requirements— (a) ensure that information in relation to each person on board is collected before the ship departs from the landing point and shall include— (i) family name; (ii) prenames or initials; (iii) gender; (iv) nationality; (V) an indication of the category of age such category being either adult, child or infant; or (vi) the age; or (vii) year of birth. (b) communicate collected information to the passenger registrar within fifteen minutes of the ship’s departure from the landing point .

provisions.title: Non-Tanzanian ships on voyages to the United Republic of Tanzania
provisions.content: The owner of a passenger ship which is not a Tanzanian ship on a voyage from any landing point outside Tanzania shall make information as to the total number of persons on board the ship and information specified in regulation 7(1) , available to the appropriate search and rescue services for the purposes of search and rescue in the event of an emergency or in the aftermath of an accident involving the ship.

provisions.title: Establishment of a registration system
provisions.content: (1) The owner of a passenger ship to which these Regulations apply shall— (a) establish a system for registration of the information collected pursuant to these Regulations to be approved by the Registrar of Ships ; (b) appoint a shore-based passenger registrar who shall be responsible for holding the information collected pursuant to these Regulations and for its transmission to the appropriate search and rescue services in the event of an emergency or in the aftermath of an accident involving the ship; (c) ensure that the information collected pursuant to these Regulations is at all times available for transmission to the appropriate search and rescue services for use in an emergency or in the aftermath of an accident involving the ship.

provisions.title: Check on registration system
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar of Ships may appoint persons to carry out checks on the proper functioning of registration systems approved under these Regulations. (2) In performing the checks referred to under sub-regulation (1) , the persons appointed shall— (a) be entitled to board any ship to which the registration system relates and enter any premises of the passenger registrar ; and (b) have access to any records and documents, including electronic and computer records, which comprise the owner's registration system.

provisions.title: False statements
provisions.content: A person shall not in connection with a system of counting of persons on board a passenger ship or collecting the information specified in regulation 9 (1)— (a) knowingly or recklessly make any false statement intended to or leading to error in the determination of the total number of persons on board or in the collection of the information specified in regulation (1) ; (b) falsify the information collected or transmitted to the passenger registrar . Part III – Offences and penalties

provisions.title: Offences
provisions.content: (1) Any person who contravenes the provisions of Regulation 5 and any other provision of these Regulations in respect of a passenger ship commits an offence and upon conviction, is liable to a fine of not less than the equivalent in Tanzanian shillings of the United States dollars five thousand or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or to both. (2) Any person who furnishes to the Registrar of Ships any record, statement, declaration, written information or explanation containing any particulars which to his knowledge is false or incorrect commits an offence.

provisions.title: Power to compound offence
provisions.content: (1) Notwithstanding the provisions of these Regulations relating to penalty, where a person has committed any offence under these Regulations the Authority may, at any time prior to the commencement of hearing by any court of any charge in relation thereto, compound such offence and order such person to pay such sum of money, not exceeding one half of the amount of the fine to which such person would otherwise have been liable if he had been convicted of such offence: Provided that the Authority shall not exercise its powers under this regulation unless the person concerned admits in writing that he has committed such offence and requests that it be compounded. (2) Where the Authority compounds offence under this regulation the order referred to in sub-regulation (1) — (a) shall be reduced to writing and there shall be attached to it the written admission and request referred to in the provision to the said sub-regulation and a copy of such order shall be given if he so requests to the person who committed the offence; (b) shall specify the offence committed, the sum of money ordered to be paid and the date or dates on which payment is to be settled; (c) shall be final and shall not be subject to any appeal to any court; (d) may be enforced in the same manner as a decree of a court for the payment of the amount stated in the order. (3) On the payment of any sum of money under subsection (1) — (a) the person who committed the offence in respect of which the payment has been made shall, if in custody, be discharged; (b) any ship detained in respect of such offence shall be released; and (c) no further proceedings shall be taken against such person or ship in respect of such offence.
